response.justification: Bogdan brings 19+ years of software engineering experience with proven expertise in high-performance systems, event-driven architectures, and disciplined engineering practices—capabilities that could theoretically transfer to C++ development. However, his C++ experience is minimal (6 months at National Instruments in 2015) and he has spent the last decade primarily on TypeScript, Node.js, and D language development. For a mid-senior C++ role requiring hands-on architectural design and performance optimization, the gap in recent C++ practice is significant. Additionally, there is no evidence of IoT, industrial automation, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL) knowledge—critical to the role's security and domain requirements. The 7-month career gap (Oct 2025–present) is also notable.
strengths: 19+ years of software engineering experience with proven ability to build low-latency, high-performance distributed systems (Dunnhumby experience with real-time metadata handling)
Demonstrated expertise in event-driven architectures and message-driven design (GISCollective platform, microservices at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, Refactoring Dojos) and mentoring track record (Selenium course, code kata sessions)
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments in 2015; no hands-on C++ work in past decade). Recent 10 years dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and D language.
No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT, or security standards (IEC 62443, SDL, secure development lifecycle). All background is web/backend systems.
Missing industrial protocol knowledge (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, IEC 104, DNP 3.0, OPC UA) and no evidence of on-premises automation machinery systems work.

key_factors.factor: Years of software engineering experience and seniority level
Missing industrial automation and cybersecurity standards domain knowledge
